Filer Trust Lanes

The CC-adoption issue filer used to file on gap_score alone: 46 of 49 filed issues were keyword false positives, while a real adoption (the 2.1.214 SessionStart fork-source feature, score 5, no affected_skills) was never filed. These lanes gate filing on codebase evidence — does the plugin tree actually reference the surface? Drag the inputs to see where a feature lands.

Precision gate #2993 · sub-floor recall #2992 · cap-saturation aid #2950 — scripts/cc-file-adoption-issues.sh

Simulated feature

5
Evidence hit a surface token appears in the plugin tree

Presets

FILED

Recall lane

disposition: filed (recall)

⚠️ cap-saturation: at gap_score 20 this feature sits at the extractor hard cap — a 20-feature release may have had additional features truncated (cap_saturated stamped, #2950).

Decision path

Precision — filed
(issue created)

Score ≥ floor and the tree references the surface. Files through the normal create path.

Precision — noop
triaged-noop-no-evidence

Score ≥ floor but no plugin-tree reference. Not ours to adopt — skipped, stamped, logged.

Recall — filed
(issue created, recall note)

Sub-floor score but evidence exists. The independent safety net that catches empty-affected_skills adoptions.

Recall — unfiled
unfiled-subfloor

Sub-floor and no evidence. Recorded so the miss is visible — never a silent drop.

Floor = MIN_GAP_SCORE (10). Recall band = [RECALL_MIN, floor) = [4, 10). Below 4 is too weak to file even with evidence. Toggle the gate off with ORK_EVIDENCE_GATE=0 to fall back to legacy score-only filing.
